title: Robertson's Real Problem Test
content: Biggest risk in software: building the wrong thing. Test: ask 'Why do you need this?' before writing any requirement. Stakeholder says 'I need XYZ' → BA discovers real problem is ABC → propose MVP → test → learn → iterate.
tags: real problem test robertson why discovery root cause

title: Value Triangle
content: MVP sits at intersection of three forces: Business Value (ROI justification); Customer Need (what users actually need); Technical Feasibility (what can be built). Features outside this intersection waste resources.
tags: value triangle MVP business value customer need technical feasibility

title: Just-in-Time Requirements
content: Do not write all requirements upfront. Clarify requirements only for next 1-2 sprints. Maintain continuous backlog refinement. Reduces wasted analysis on features that change or are cut. Defer decisions until last responsible moment.
tags: just-in-time requirements JIT backlog refinement sprint defer

title: User Story Mapping Procedure
content: 1) Identify user activities as backbone (horizontal axis); 2) Break each activity into user tasks; 3) Arrange tasks by priority vertically; 4) Draw MVP line — everything above = Release 1; 5) Group lower rows into subsequent releases. Reveals scope and release slices.
tags: user story mapping backbone activities tasks MVP release slice

title: Hypothesis-Driven Development Template
content: Template: 'We believe [feature] will achieve [outcome] for [user]. We will know this is true when [measurable metric].' Forces teams to define success before building. Prevents building features with no success criteria.
tags: hypothesis driven development template outcome metric feature

title: Build-Measure-Learn Loop
content: Lean Startup cycle: BUILD (create smallest testable increment); MEASURE (collect data on real usage); LEARN (decide: pivot or persevere). Repeat. Minimizes time and cost of learning what customers actually value.
tags: build measure learn loop lean startup pivot persevere

title: Agile BA Checklist
content: Before sprint: 1) Asked 'Why?' before writing requirement? 2) Defined MVP? 3) Created User Story Map? 4) Written Hypothesis for new features? 5) Planned how to measure outcomes? All 5 must be true before development begins.
tags: agile BA checklist sprint planning hypothesis story map MVP measurement

title: Risk-Driven vs Change-Driven Calibration
content: Low uncertainty → Detailed specs acceptable (Waterfall OK). Medium uncertainty → Iterative development with prototypes and MVPs. High uncertainty → Experiments, spikes, A/B tests. Match process rigor to actual uncertainty level.
tags: risk driven change driven uncertainty calibration waterfall agile experiment

title: Timebox Iteration Pattern
content: Fix the time (sprint duration); vary the scope. Forces prioritization. Each iteration must produce working software. Review and adapt at end of each timebox. Prevents scope creep by making trade-offs explicit each sprint.
tags: timebox iteration sprint fixed time variable scope prioritization

title: Agile Elicitation Approach
content: Progressive elaboration: broad strokes first (Epic → Story → Task). Use lightweight techniques: story writing workshops; example mapping; BDD scenarios. Avoid big upfront analysis. Validate assumptions through rapid prototypes.
tags: agile elicitation progressive elaboration epic story BDD example mapping prototype

title: Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
content: Smallest increment that delivers enough value to learn from real users. Not a minimal product — an experiment. Must have: clear hypothesis; defined success metric; real users; feedback loop. Different from prototype (throwaway) or pilot (limited rollout).
tags: MVP minimum viable product experiment hypothesis success metric feedback

title: Backlog Refinement Pattern
content: Ongoing activity (not a one-time meeting). Refine top backlog items to ready state: clear acceptance criteria; estimated; small enough for one sprint. Recommended: 10% of sprint capacity. Ensures sprint planning runs smoothly.
tags: backlog refinement grooming acceptance criteria estimation ready sprint

title: Agile BA Role Definition
content: BA in Agile bridges product owner and development team. Responsibilities: decompose epics into stories; facilitate example mapping; create lightweight models; validate acceptance criteria; support sprint reviews. Not a documentation-first role.
tags: agile BA role product owner bridge decompose stories models acceptance criteria

title: Example Mapping Technique
content: Workshop format for refining user stories: Yellow cards = Story; Blue cards = Rules (acceptance criteria); Green cards = Examples (concrete scenarios); Red cards = Questions (unknowns). Session ends when no red cards remain or questions are parked.
tags: example mapping story rules examples questions acceptance criteria workshop

title: Spike Pattern
content: Time-boxed research task to reduce technical or requirements uncertainty. Output is learning (not working code). Define: question to answer; time-box duration; definition of done. Schedule spike before committing to implementation estimate.
tags: spike research uncertainty time-box learning technical discovery
